about.label: About
about.summary: I make games, prototypes, and weird little systems that somehow start as "this should be quick" and end with me happily deep in architecture, tools, and backend logic.
body: I have been working with Unity for more than a decade, which is long enough to have made a lot of things, broken a lot of things, and become suspicious whenever someone says a feature is just a simple little addition.

Over the years I have gone from self-directed experimentation and small projects into building more reusable gameplay systems, better workflows, and tools that help teams move faster without setting the whole project on fire.

Most of my work lives in the space between programming and design. I like solving the practical problems that make games feel better to build and better to play, whether that is gameplay architecture, iteration speed, multiplayer logic, or backend systems that do their job quietly in the background.
I have worked across personal projects, team projects, jam games, and shipped releases, and I tend to gravitate toward the technical glue that holds everything together.
I have also recently started my Advanced Diploma in Game Design, so alongside building games professionally and independently, I am still actively sharpening the design side of my work too.

entries.label: Early Years
entries.title: Learning By Making Questionable Things
entries.body: This was the era of making a lot of projects, breaking a lot of projects, and accidentally learning a huge amount of Unity by refusing to stop poking at it.

entries.label: Systems Focus
entries.title: From Random Prototypes to Systems That Behave Themselves
entries.body: I started caring a lot more about structure, reusable systems, and workflows that let teams move quickly without every feature turning into a mystery box.

entries.label: Shipped Work
entries.title: Turning That Into Actual Shipped Work
entries.body: That technical foundation turned into shipped games, team collaboration, and the kind of practical problem solving that only shows up when deadlines and real players are involved.

entries.label: Now
entries.title: Still Building, Still Iterating, Slightly Wiser
entries.body: Right now I am still building technically grounded interactive work with a focus on clarity, feel, and scalable design, while also starting my Advanced Diploma in Game Design and continuing to sharpen the design side of what I do.

contact.label: Contact
contact.body: Actively looking for new opportunities as a Unity Programmer or Game Designer.

projects.label: Carrot Commotion
projects.title: Carrot Commotion
projects.summary: A social deduction party game for up to eight players built around hidden information, shifting identities, and readable multiplayer systems.

projects.label: Ridiculous Gungame
projects.title: Ridiculous Gungame
projects.summary: An instagib party shooter built around ridiculous weapons and fast, chaotic multiplayer matches.

projects.label: Balls to the Walls
projects.title: Balls to the Walls
projects.summary: An incremental brick-breaking prototype built as a playground for progression systems, player feedback, and satisfying moment-to-moment feel.

projects.label: Baroquen Bones
projects.title: Baroquen Bones
projects.summary: A stylised prototype focused on strong visual direction, readable mechanics, and the kind of experimentation that starts with one idea and somehow becomes five systems.

projects.label: I Have More Hours Than You
projects.title: I Have More Hours Than You
projects.summary: An abstract idle-like experience that uses Steam achievements as a core part of both progression and narrative framing.

projects.label: One Ships Trash
projects.title: One Ships Trash
projects.summary: A magnet-fishing jam game about cleaning an underwater reef, designed and built in two (2) days.

projects.label: Dino Eco Simulation
projects.title: Dino Eco Simulation
projects.summary: A relaxed ecosystem simulation focused on observing dinosaur life cycles instead of controlling or fighting them.

projects.label: Who's Your Possessor
projects.title: Who's Your Possessor
projects.summary: A possession-based prototype built around playful chaos, clear interactions, and a strong core gimmick with immediate payoff.

projects.label: Mandela Protocol
projects.title: Mandela Protocol
projects.summary: An anomaly hunting game set in a robot-lead world. Players take on the role of a scientist, using a camera to gather information for our overseer.
